```{r data generation, echo = FALSE, results = "hide", message=F, warning=F}
library(exams)
library(tidyverse)


new_url <- "https://www.atu.ie/"

answer_list <- tribble(~feature, ~node,
                       "Student Hub", ".menu-item-47 a",
                       "Staff Hub", ".menu-item-48 a",
                       "Events", ".menu-item-49 a",
                       "Connect", ".menu-item-55 .has-menu",
                       "Upcoming Events", ".m-b-1",
                       "Research and Innovation", ".cell:nth-child(2) li:nth-child(1) .text",
                       "IT Services", ".cell:nth-child(1) li:nth-child(3) .text",
                       "Campus Locations", ".hide-for-small-only .text"
                       )



index <- sample(1:8, 1)
other_index <- c(1:8)[!(1:8 %in% index)]
question <- answer_list$feature[index]
answer <- answer_list$node[index]

other_answers <- answer_list$node[other_index] |> 
  sample(5)

solution <- c(answer, other_answers)

```


Question
========
Examine the ATU webpage for  __`r new_url`__. Which is the correct __css__ selector for __`r question`__? 

Answerlist
----------
* `r solution[1]`
* `r solution[2]`
* `r solution[3]`
* `r solution[4]`
* `r solution[5]`
* `r solution[6]`


Solution
========
Either use `Ctrl+U` to examine the html code for the webpage, or more simply, use the __*Selector Gagdet*__ browser plugin.

Answerlist
----------
* True
* False
* False
* False
* False
* False


Meta-information
================
exname: R selector-gadget
extype: schoice
exsolution: 100000
exshuffle: 5
